LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CAVCO INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED AND MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF'S INTERROGATORY RESPONSES SPECIFICALLY DESCRIBE THE TYPE OF WORK HE PERFORMED, A DISCRETE TIME PERIOD, AND THE PRODUCTS HE WORKED WITH. PLAINTIFF TESTIFIED HE WORKED ON ORIGINAL ROOF COATING AND THAT DEFENDANT'S MOBILE HOMES WERE COMMON. (PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION PAGES 94-95 AND 113.) DEFENDANT DOES NOT PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT ITS ORIGINAL ROOF COATINGS DID NOT CONTAIN ASBESTOS. HEVENER DEPOSITION CITES KOOL SEAL AND ELIXIR ROOF COATINGS AS CONTAINING ASBESTOS. OBJECTIONS TO ALL DECLARATIONS SUSTAINED FOR LACK OF PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE AND INSUFFICIENT FOUNDATION. SOPHISTICATED USER DEFENSE NOT APPLICABLE, NO EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF WAS TRAINED OR "SOPHISTICATED." ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

ASBESTOS MOTION CALENDAR ON JUL-28-2009 IN DEPT. 206, PLAINTIFF GREGORY MILBURN AND LIZA MILBURN'S MOTION TO SPECIALLY SET A TRIAL DATE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. 36 (D). THE TRIAL DATE IS SET FOR 11/16/09 @ 11:15AM IN DEPT. 206. PLAINTIFF TO CONTACT DEPT. 502 FOR SETTLEMENT CONFERENCES AND SEND NOTICE. DISCOVERY IS OPEN UNTIL THE CASE IS ASSIGNED TO A TRIAL COURT. ALL DISCOVERY TO BE NOTICED AND HEARD IN DEPT. 206. G.O. 140 RE: MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT WAS STIPULATED TO. PLAINTIFF SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL IDENTIFICATION AND LAY WITNESSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS THAT SUPPORT THEIR TESTIMONY, AS TO EACH INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT BY 30 DAYS AFTER THE GRANTING OF PREFERENCE. IF PLAINTIFF LEARNS OF NEW WITNESSES AFTER THE DUE DATE THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT IDENTIFIED PREVIOUSLY. ALL DEFENDANTS SHALL DISCLOSE THE NAMES AND ADDRESSES OF ALL LAY WITNESSES THAT THE INDIVIDUAL DEFENDANT LEARNED OF THROUGH ITS OWN DISCOVERY EFFORTS AND THAT WILL SUPPORT PROPOSITION 51 PURPOSES OR FOR THEIR AFFIRMATIVE DEFENSES, AND PRODUCE ANY DOCUMENTS UPON WHICH THESE WITNESSES SUPPORT, AS TO EACH DEFENDANT BY 50 DAYS AFTER THE GRANTING OF PREFERENCE. IF DEFENDANTS LEARN OF NEW WITNESSES AND OR DOCUMENTS, IN GOOD FAITH, THEY SHALL IMMEDIATELY BE DISCLOSED ALONG WITH DECLARATION AS TO WHY THIS DISCLOSURE WAS NOT PROVIDED PREVIOUSLY. ALL ECONOMIC EXPERT DISCOVERY IS TO BE COMPLETED 14 CALENDAR DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, INCLUDING DEPOSITION OF ECONOMIC EXPERT. THE PARED-DOWN LIST OF EXPERTS WILL BE FAXED NO LATER THAN 14 DAYS BEFORE TRIAL DATE. NO LATER THAN 12 DAYS BEFORE THE TRIAL DATE, EACH SIDE MAY IDENTIFY UP TO THREE EXPERT WITNESSES PERCEIVED TO BE NECESSARY TO BE DEPOSED FOR MEANINGFUL SETTLEMENT DISCUSSIONS TO OCCUR. THESE EXPERTS WILL BE DEPOSED PRIOR TO THE TRIAL DATE. TRIAL WILL NOT BE DELAYED FOR THE PURPOSE OF DEPOSING EXPERTS. WRITTEN ORDER PENDING. JUDGE: JAMES MCBRIDE; REPORTER: BOB BALIAN

Off Calendar Oct-30-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CAVCO INDUSTRIES, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Stephen Tigerman, Esq. of Harowitz & Tigerman LLP (415) 788-1588, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Kevin R. Mintz, Esq. of Rankin, Sproat, Mires, Beaty & Reynolds, (510) 465-3922 appearing for Defendant and moving party; Carey L. Cooper, Esq. of Klinedinst, P.C. Appearing thru CourtCall for Defendant Champion. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: MOTION DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT BURDEN OF PROOF. PLAINTIFF'S INTERROGATORY RESPONSES SPECIFICALLY DESCRIBE THE TYPE OF WORK HE PERFORMED, A DISCRETE TIME PERIOD, AND THE PRODUCTS HE WORKED WITH. PLAINTIFF TESTIFIED HE WORKED ON ORIGINAL ROOF COATING AND THAT DEFENDANT'S MOBILE HOMES WERE COMMON. (PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION PAGES 94-95 AND 113.) DEFENDANT DOES NOT PRESENT EVIDENCE THAT ITS ORIGINAL ROOF COATINGS DID NOT CONTAIN ASBESTOS. HEVENER DEPOSITION CITES KOOL SEAL AND ELIXIR ROOF COATINGS AS CONTAINING ASBESTOS. OBJECTIONS TO ALL DECLARATIONS SUSTAINED FOR LACK OF PERSONAL KNOWLEDGE AND INSUFFICIENT FOUNDATION. SOPHISTICATED USER DEFENSE NOT APPLICABLE, NO EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF WAS TRAINED OR "SOPHISTICATED." Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797
